steps.title: Use the evidence gap
steps.content: Historians of capitalism often rely on longitudinal economic data from archival records, but those records do not exist for much of precolonial Africa. Pereira and other Africanist scholars still found evidence of capitalism in that period.

steps.title: Reject unsupported differences
steps.content: B assumes both groups use archival records, even though the passage says those records are largely absent. C invents a disagreement over what counts as capitalism. D invents a view about capitalism's benefits. The answer is A.
